quoteFirst: After dinner he always had a nap with the Globe newspaper on his knee, and his yellow bandanna handkerchief on his face (Major Pendennis sent the yellow handkerchiefs from India, and his brother had helped in the purchase of his majority, so that they were good friends now). And so, as his dinner took place at six o’clock to a minute, and the sunset business alluded to may be supposed to have occurred at about
quoteLast: , it is probable that he did not much care for the view in front of his lawn windows or take any share in the poetry and caresses which were taking place there.
title: The History of Pendennis
author: Thackeray, William Makepeace, 1811-1863

quoteLast: , Roncherolle was up and dressed and was cleaning a pair of pistols, which, despite his destitution, he had always kept. At a few minutes before eight, the door was opened softly and Chicotin appeared, accompanied by Georget.
title: The Flower Girl of The Château d'Eau, v.2 (Novels of Paul de Kock Volume XVI)
author: Kock, Paul de, 1793-1871

quoteLast: , went to Caserta, and walked over the palace, in which nothing struck me but the dimensions, the staircase, and a few of the rooms. The theatre is very well contrived; it is at one end of the palace, and the back of it opens by large folding doors into the garden, so that they can have any depth of stage they please, and arrange any pageants or cavalcades.
title:
The Greville Memoirs, Part 1 (of 3), Volume 1 (of 3)
A Journal of the Reigns of King George IV and King William IV
author: Greville, Charles, 1794-1865; Reeve, Henry, 1813-1895 [Editor]

quoteLast: ,” said Archie, leaving him, “and dinner will be served at a quarter to eight. I’ve never been late but once, and Achille was so hurt that he gave me notice. I promised that it should never occur again, and it hasn’t. He doesn’t insist on my dressing for dinner, though he says he should like it.”
title: A Gray Eye or So. In Three Volumes—Volume II
author: Moore, Frank Frankfort, 1855-1931

quoteLast: next day, we made short work of the march to the railway, which we struck at Frederickstadt, a place that many of us were destined to become very well acquainted with before we had done. It is rather prettier than most Boer villages, being situated on the pleasant little Mooi River, whose clear, rapid current reminded us of our home streams.
title:
The Second Battalion Royal Dublin Fusiliers in the South African War
With a Description of the Operations in the Aden Hinterland
author: Romer, Cecil Francis, 1869-; Mainwaring, Arthur Edward, 1864-

quoteLast: when we arrived back at King's Cross, and after a hasty chop at a small Italian restaurant opposite the station, we both drove to Grosvenor Square, in order to explain to Mabel our success in the solution of the cipher.
title: As We Forgive Them
author: Le Queux, William, 1864-1927

quoteFirst: To see the Gitanas at their best, or living under primitive conditions, take a trip down to Coria on the Guadalquivir. A steamboat starts daily from the Triana Bridge at about
quoteLast: . The voyage is interesting, and you can return in time for evening dinner. You pass two or three villages with landing-stages, and gain views of the distant marshes towards the mouth of the river, while on the right bank are slopes clothed with olives and vines.
title: The Story of Seville
author: Gallichan, Walter M. (Walter Matthew), 1861-1946; Hartley, C. Gasquoine (Catherine Gasquoine), 1867-1928 [Contributor]

quoteLast: when Mother was well," she answered, "but since she was sick, Shirley got in the habit of staying up till Sarah goes and sometimes Sarah won't go till I do."
